# XMAD Next.js Dashboard Implementation Plan

**Source:** Generated by Claude Website
 consolidated by Claude Code CLI (GLM-5)
 audited by Claude Opus 4.6

This document provides a complete implementation plan for the XMAD Control Center dashboard.

---

## Overview

This plan consists of three parts:
- **Part 1.1**: Initial Next.js web UI implementation (simpler version)
- **Part 1.2**: Enhanced implementation with security, SSE, and full component library
- **Part 2**: Handoff instructions for implementation

## Architecture

- Next.js 15.x frontend
- Proxy layer to existing backend (port 8765)
- Server-Sent Events for live stats
- Tailwind v4 CSS-first configuration
- noVNC via websockify iframe integration
- Tailscale IP restriction middleware
- macOS Keychain for secrets (no .env files)

## Part 1.1 - Basic Implementation (FILES 1-17)

### Files Included:
1. `package.json` - Dependencies
2. `next.config.ts` - Proxy configuration
3. `postcss.config.mjs` - Tailwind setup
4. `app/globals.css` - CSS variables
5. `app/layout.tsx` - Root layout with Sidebar
6. `components/Sidebar.tsx` - Navigation component
7. `components/StatusBadge.tsx` - Health status display
8. `app/page.tsx` - Dashboard page
9. `app/nova/page.tsx` - Nova chat interface
10. `app/memory/page.tsx` - Brain file editor
11. `app/screen/page.tsx` - Screen viewer
12. `app/automation/page.tsx` - Job queue
13. `app/backups/page.tsx` - Backup manager
14. `app/settings/page.tsx` - Settings page
15. `com.xmad.web.plist` - LaunchAgent
16. `install.sh` - Installation script
17. `tsconfig.json` - TypeScript config
18. `biome.json` - Linting/formatting

## Part 1.2 - Enhanced Implementation (FILES 1-34)

### Enhanced Files:
1. `types/xmad.ts` - Central type definitions
2. `lib/security.ts` - Path traversal prevention, IP validation
3. `lib/secrets.ts` - SSOT secrets loader
4. `lib/xmad-api.ts` - API client with gateway fallback
5. `lib/sse.ts` - SSE stream factory
6. `hooks/use-sse.ts` - SSE client hook

15. `components/widgets/alert-banner.tsx` - Alert display
16. `app/api/xmad/[...path]/route.ts` - API proxy
17. `app/api/xmad/events/route.ts` - SSE endpoint
18. `middleware.ts` - Tailscale IP guard
19. `app/layout.tsx` - Root layout with AppShell
20. `app/globals.css` - CSS variables

22. `app/page.tsx` - Dashboard with SSE
23. `app/chat/page.tsx` - Chat interface

31. `app/terminal/page.tsx` - Log viewer
32. `public/manifest.json` - PWA manifest
33. `scripts/load-secrets.sh` - Keychain loader
34. `com.xmad.web.plist` - Updated LaunchAgent

## Part 2 - Implementation Instructions

### Steps:
1. Audit current project structure
2. Install dependencies
3. Create all directories
4. Verify critical files
5. Type check
6. Build check
7. Verify secrets script
8. Install LaunchAgent
9. End-to-end connectivity test
10. Generate audit report

## Key Design Decisions

1. API proxy at /api/xmad/[...path] - All backend calls through Next.js
2. Middleware Tailscale guard - Network-layer security only
3. Memory editor allowlist - Exact path match only
4. SSE route force-dynamic - Prevents static rendering
5. Secrets via Keychain - No .env files in codebase

## Ports Reference

| Service            | Port  | Notes                                    |
|--------------------|------|------------------------------------------|
| xmad-web (Next.js) | 3000 | This project                             |
| XMAD Gateway       | 9870 | New — built by OpenClaw agent          |
| XMAD Legacy API    | 8765 | Existing server.js — kept as fallback |
| OpenClaw           | 18789 | Nova AI runtime                          |
| noVNC              | 6080 | Screen sharing iframe target             |

## Memory Optimization (Part 3)

### Target: <400MB total RAM

### Principles:
1. Process isolation - Worker processes for heavy services
2. Lazy loading - Load modules on needed
3. Streaming - Avoid full buffering
4. Memory-efficient caching - LRU caches
5. Garbage collection - Periodic cleanup

### Module Strategy:
- OpenClaw Core: Worker process (~150MB)
- AI Chat: Load models on demand
- Memory Editor: Streaming edits
- Automation Queue: Worker pool (2-3 workers)
- Backup Manager: Stream to disk
- Logs/Events: SSE streaming (last 200 lines in memory)
- VNC Viewer: Proxy only

## AUDIT RESULTS (2026-03-19)

### Summary

The current xmad-control implementation is **MORE ADVANCED** than this plan describes:

| Metric | Plan | Actual |
|--------|------|--------|
| Components | ~30 | 143 |
| Architecture | Simple pages | Surface-based with lazy loading |
| UI Library | Basic styled | Full Glass UI (22 components) |
| Voice | Not mentioned | Full STT/TTS integration |
| Task System | Basic queue | Resource governance + events |

### Implementation Status

| Part | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| Part 1.1 | ✅ 95% | Core pages implemented as surfaces |
| Part 1.2 | ⚠️ 70% | Missing SSE endpoint, catch-all proxy |
| Part 2 | N/A | Instructions for implementation |

### Missing Items (High Priority)

1. `/api/xmad/[...path]/route.ts` - Catch-all proxy route
2. `/api/xmad/events/route.ts` - SSE events endpoint
3. `lib/security.ts` - Path traversal validation
4. `components/widgets/tailscale-widget.tsx` - VPN status
5. `hooks/use-sse.ts` - SSE client hook

### Items Beyond Plan Scope

- Voice system (STT/TTS APIs)
- Task supervisor with resource governance
- Full Glass UI component library
- Registry system for reusable components
- PWA components

### Verdict

**Plan is OUTDATED** - The project has evolved significantly. Recommend:
1. Update plan to reflect surface-based architecture
2. Implement missing SSE and security items
3. Document actual architecture in plan

See `xmad-nextjs-dashboard-audit-report.md` for full details.
